task_completion.description: Open the corrupted IFC model in the BIM software.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender/Bonsai interface is open, and the outliner or window title indicates that 'fzk_proxy_corrupted.ifc' has been loaded. The 3D viewport should display the house model.

task_completion.description: Reassign generic proxy elements representing walls to the IfcWall class.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Blender outliner or Bonsai Object Properties panel (IFC Object Metadata), elements with descriptive names indicating they are walls are shown with their IFC Class changed from 'IfcBuildingElementProxy' to 'IfcWall'.

task_completion.description: Reassign generic proxy elements representing windows to the IfcWindow class.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Blender outliner or Bonsai Object Properties panel, elements with descriptive names indicating they are windows are shown with their IFC Class changed to 'IfcWindow'.

task_completion.description: Reassign generic proxy elements representing doors to the IfcDoor class.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Blender outliner or Bonsai Object Properties panel, elements with descriptive names indicating they are doors are shown with their IFC Class changed to 'IfcDoor'.

task_completion.description: Reassign generic proxy elements representing slabs to the IfcSlab class.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Blender outliner or Bonsai Object Properties panel, elements with descriptive names indicating they are slabs are shown with their IFC Class changed to 'IfcSlab'.

task_completion.description: Save the corrected IFC project to the specified output path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file save/export dialog or a confirmation message showing the project being saved as an IFC file to the exact path '/home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_remediated.ifc'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Blender/Bonsai graphical user interface to perform the semantic remediation.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through the Blender interface, using the outliner, and modifying IFC classes in the Object Properties panel, rather than just executing a Python script in the terminal.

integrity.description: The original geometry of the model is preserved during the reassignment process.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D viewport continues to display the full architectural shell of the FZK-Haus without elements disappearing, moving, or becoming distorted after their classes are changed.

integrity.description: The agent saved the file using the application's export/save functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Blender file browser or Bonsai export dialog is visible, proving the file was saved from the application and not just copied/renamed via terminal commands.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The corrupted model is located at '/home/ga/IFCModels/fzk_proxy_corrupted.ifc'. The target output file must be saved to '/home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_remediated.ifc'. A perfectly remediated file will contain exactly 33 reassigned elements: 13 IfcWall, 11 IfcWindow, 5 IfcDoor, and 4 IfcSlab elements. The agent must use the BlenderBIM/Bonsai GUI to perform these reassignments.
